description: Pinecrest on Lotela sits on the south shore of Lake Lotela in Avon Park and carries one of Florida's most storied pedigrees — Donald Ross consulted on the layout in 1926, making it a centerpiece of the Florida Historic Golf Trail. The Donald Ross-influenced design rewards shot-makers with its classic undulating greens and strategic bunkering.
notes: Florida Historic Golf Trail course; Donald Ross 1926

description: River Greens Golf Course is a family-owned 18-hole layout situated along Lake Damon in Avon Park, designed by Jack Kidwell in 1969. The course offers affordable daily-fee play in a relaxed, no-frills environment popular with Highlands County locals.

Babcock National is an 18-hole Gordon B. Lewis design opened in 2020 within the solar-powered Babcock Ranch community northeast of Punta Gorda. The 7,176-yard layout weaves around numerous lakes while offering generous landing zones, making water a constant but manageable factor on virtually every hole.
notes: Located within Babcock Ranch master-planned community; Charlotte County

description: Twisted Oaks Golf Club in Beverly Hills is a Karl Litten-designed layout from 1990 that stands out in Citrus County for its unusually rolling terrain — a rarity in Central Florida. The links-style routing across Forest Ridge offers a more topographically interesting round than most courses in the region.
notes: Architect Karl Litten; rolling terrain uncommon for region

description: Brentwood at Citrus Hills is a 9-hole executive course within the Villages of Citrus Hills residential community in Lecanto, offering a quicker round over rolling Citrus County terrain. Smaller greens and a compact layout make it well-suited for residents seeking a relaxed game without the time commitment of 18 holes.
notes: 9-hole executive course within Citrus Hills community; address is Lecanto not Beverly Hills

description: The Quarry Course at Black Diamond Ranch is one of Florida's most celebrated private layouts, a Tom Fazio design from 1987 that routes through two abandoned limestone quarries. Five holes descend into the dramatic quarry walls, creating elevation changes and scenery found nowhere else in the state.
notes: Consistently ranked among Florida's top private courses; Tom Fazio 1987

description: The Ranch Course at Black Diamond Ranch is Tom Fazio's second design on the property, opening in 1997 across rolling woodland terrain northwest of the quarry land. The layout delivers a more classical parkland experience, complementing the dramatic quarry routing next door within this exclusive private Citrus County club.
notes: Tom Fazio 1997; companion course to the Quarry

description: Citrus Hills Golf & Country Club in Hernando features three 18-hole championship courses — the Skyview, Oaks, and Meadows — spread across Citrus County's rolling Nature Coast terrain. The facility has been the anchor of the Villages of Citrus Hills residential community since 1983 and welcomes outside play.
notes: Three 18-hole courses; facility address is Hernando, FL not Citrus Hills

description: Citrus Springs Golf & Country Club is an 18-hole layout established in 1972 within the Citrus Springs residential community of Citrus County. The course offers straightforward Florida-style play on a well-maintained track that has served the local community for more than five decades.

The Ridge Course at Kings Ridge Golf Club is the executive companion to the championship Kings Course, added in 2002 within the Kings Ridge active-adult community in Clermont. The shorter layout is designed for quicker daily rounds and caters primarily to Kings Ridge residents.
notes: Executive course; resident-focused community club

description: Clermont National (formerly Sanctuary Ridge) is a public 18-hole course in Clermont that reopened in 2024 following an extensive $3 million renovation. Perched on one of Lake County's highest ridges, the layout delivers elevation changes unusual for Central Florida golf.
notes: Formerly Sanctuary Ridge; reopened as Clermont National after 2023-24 renovation

description: Swiss Fairways Country Club is a 9-hole course established in 1991 within a residential community near Lake Minneola in Clermont. The layout occupies a compact footprint and is popular with members seeking a low-key, affordable game in Lake County.
notes: 9-hole course

description: Kings Ridge Golf Club's championship Kings Course opened in 1996 within Clermont's premiere active-adult community, offering an 18-hole layout designed for daily play by community residents. The course winds through the gated Kings Ridge neighborhood in Lake County's rolling terrain.
notes: Championship 18-hole Kings Course; resident-focused community club

description: Clewiston Golf Course is a municipal 18-hole layout owned and operated by the City of Clewiston that first opened in 1929 on the south shore of Lake Okeechobee. One of Florida's older public tracks, it offers open play to all visitors and remains a community anchor in Hendry County.
notes: Municipal course; Florida Historic Golf Trail

description: Rainbow's End Golf Club is a long-established 18-hole course tucked into the rural western edge of Marion County near Dunnellon. The mature, tree-lined layout has served the local community since around 1960 and offers affordable play in a quiet, unhurried setting.
notes: Year established approximate; some sources cite 1965

description: Juliette Falls Golf Club is an 18-hole Ian Baker-Finch design that debuted in 2008 about 20 miles west of Ocala, earning a Golf Digest top-five new course ranking in its opening year. The layout features dramatic elevation changes rare in Florida, with the Rainbow Springs corridor providing a memorable natural backdrop.
notes: Golf Digest top-5 new course 2008; Ian Baker-Finch design

description: The Ocean Course at Hammock Beach Resort is a Jack Nicklaus Signature design that opened in 2000 as the first oceanfront course built in Florida in over 70 years. Six holes play directly along the Atlantic dunes, with the four-hole closing stretch nicknamed 'The Bear Claw' providing one of the most dramatic finishes on Florida's east coast.
notes: Jack Nicklaus Signature design; Flagler County

description: One of The Villages' resident-only executive courses set within the sprawling retirement community spanning Lake, Marion, and Sumter counties in Central Florida. The layout provides a short, relaxed round designed for daily play and is accessible exclusively to Village residents and their registered guests.
notes: The Villages resident-only executive course
Driving range only; 10810 S Suncoast Blvd, Homosassa FL 34446

description: Bishops Gate Golf and Lakeside Community in Howey-in-the-Hills is a private residential golf community built around a course originally opened in 1987 (formerly known as Sarabande Country Club). The layout was renovated in 2012-13 to serve as an elite junior golf training facility in partnership with the International Junior Golf Academy.
notes: Formerly Sarabande Country Club; IJGA training facility partnership

description: El Campeon at Mission Resort + Club is one of Florida's oldest golf courses, laid out in 1917 across the rolling hills of Howey-in-the-Hills in Lake County. The historic 18-hole layout winds through ancient live oaks and citrus groves, offering a distinctly old-Florida character rarely found on courses this age.
notes: One of Florida's oldest courses; Florida Historic Golf Trail

description: Lakes of Lady Lake Golf Course is an 18-hole public layout opened in 1995 in Lady Lake, just south of The Villages corridor in Lake County. The course offers affordable daily-fee play around the namesake lakes, with easy access for residents and visitors to the greater Villages area.

The Country Club of Mount Dora is an 18-hole semi-private course opened in 1991 within a residential community on the outskirts of historic Mount Dora in Lake County. The layout blends graceful elevation changes and lake views characteristic of the Lake County ridge area.
notes: City listed as Lake County; actual city is Mount Dora
Not a golf course — East Lake Community Church

description: Placid Lakes Country Club is a community-anchored 18-hole course developed in the mid-1960s on the shores of Lake Placid and Lake June in Highlands County. The layout offers public daily-fee play alongside membership, with two scenic lakes framing several holes across the parkland terrain.
notes: Year approximate; developed mid-1960s

description: Lake Wales Country Club is a Donald Ross design from 1926, one of the architect's signature Central Florida works. The private 18-hole layout has retained much of its original strategic character across its century of play, making it a quiet gem of Florida golf heritage in Polk County.
notes: Donald Ross 1926; historic course

description: Grasslands Golf & Country Club is a purely private, member-owned 18-hole course designed by Jerry Pate and Bob Cupp, opened in 1990 within Lakeland's Oakbridge community. The club has maintained a reputation as one of Polk County's most upscale private facilities, with a 2008 course restoration by the Jerry Pate Design Group.
notes: Jerry Pate & Bob Cupp design; fully private member-owned

description: Cleveland Heights Golf Course is a public municipal layout in Lakeland that dates to 1925, making it one of Polk County's oldest continuously operating courses. The Florida Historic Golf Trail facility features three distinct 9-hole nines — Azalea, Camellia, and Magnolia — providing 27 holes of affordable public play.
notes: Florida Historic Golf Trail; 27-hole municipal course

description: Links of Sandpiper is a public 18-hole course in north Lakeland that opened in 1987, offering open daily-fee play in a relaxed Polk County setting. The layout features tree-lined fairways and a links-inspired routing that provides a reasonable challenge for players of all skill levels.

The Otter Creek Course at The Plantation at Leesburg is an 18-hole layout opened in 2014 within one of Central Florida's largest active-adult communities. The course rounds out the Plantation's two-course offering alongside the older Preserve Course, giving residents varied options for daily play.
notes: Community course within The Plantation at Leesburg

description: Arlington Ridge Golf Club is a public 18-hole course opened in 2005 within an active-adult community on the outskirts of Leesburg in Lake County. The layout provides well-maintained daily-fee play at an accessible price point, drawing both community residents and visiting golfers.

notes: Individual hole, not a standalone course — Candler Hills Hole 4
Individual hole, not a standalone course — Candler Hills Hole 1

description: Ocala Palms Golf & Country Club is a semi-private 18-hole course that opened in 1996 as the centerpiece of an active-adult community in northwest Ocala. The layout winds through the Ocala Palms residential neighborhood with lakes and mature landscaping defining most of its holes.
notes: City listed as Marion County; actual city is Ocala

description: The Links at Spruce Creek South is a semi-private 18-hole course opened in 1995 within the Spruce Creek South active-adult community in Summerfield, just south of Ocala. The course provides residents and daily-fee players a well-maintained track in a peaceful Marion County setting.
notes: City listed as Marion County; actual city is Summerfield
Individual hole, not a standalone course — Candler Hills Hole 3
Individual hole, not a standalone course — Candler Hills Hole 2

description: Mount Dora Golf Club is a public 18-hole course established in 1945 in the charming lakeside town of Mount Dora, offering affordable play on a traditional layout surrounded by the rolling hills of Lake County. The club has served as a community golf anchor for generations of Mount Dora residents.

Ocala Golf Club is one of Florida's oldest courses, dating to around 1920 on Silver Springs Boulevard in the heart of Ocala. The 18-hole public layout has been a community institution for over a century, offering accessible daily-fee play with a well-preserved classic character.
notes: One of Florida's oldest courses; circa 1920

description: Golden Ocala Golf & Equestrian Club is a Ron Garl-designed private course from 1986 that gained international attention as golf's first tribute course, featuring eight holes recreating famous moments from Augusta National, St. Andrews, and other legendary venues. The 1,200-acre gated estate in northwest Ocala pairs championship golf with a world-class equestrian center.
notes: Ron Garl design; first tribute course in golf history

description: Lake Diamond Golf & Country Club is a semi-private 18-hole course opened in 1998 within a residential community in southeastern Ocala. The layout offers daily-fee play and membership options on a well-maintained track in Marion County.

Ocala National Golf Club (at Golden Hills Country Club) is a public 18-hole course opened in 1964 in northwest Ocala, one of Marion County's longest-serving public golf facilities. The layout offers open daily-fee play on a mature course with classic Florida character.
notes: Also known as Ocala National at Golden Hills Country Club

description: SummerGlen Golf Club is an 18-hole course opened in 2000 within the SummerGlen active-adult community in southern Ocala. The community-centered layout provides daily play for residents across gently rolling terrain in Marion County, with outside play also available.

Baseline Golf Course is a public 18-hole layout opened in 1988 on the southern edge of Ocala, offering some of the most affordable green fees in Marion County. The flat, tree-lined course draws a loyal local following for its no-frills, accessible approach to daily-fee golf.

Indian Lake Estates Golf & Country Club is a semi-private 18-hole course established around 1962 within the Indian Lake Estates community in eastern Polk County. The layout sits alongside Lake Weohyakapka (Walk-in-Water) and has served this rural lakeside community for over six decades.
notes: City listed as Polk County; actual community is Indian Lake Estates

description: Lily Lake Golf Resort is a 9-hole executive layout opened in 1989 within an RV and manufactured home resort in Frostproof, Polk County. The compact course is part of an active retirement resort community and offers a quick, casual round for residents and resort guests.
notes: City listed as Polk County; actual city is Frostproof. 9-hole executive course at RV resort

description: Skyview Golf & Country Club is an 18-hole semi-private course established in 1966 in Lakeland, one of Polk County's older golf facilities. The layout offers community members and outside players a classic Florida track with mature tree-lined fairways.
notes: City listed as Polk County; actual city is Lakeland

description: The Hamptons Golf Club in Auburndale is a 9-hole executive course established in 1991 within the Hamptons Golf & Country Club manufactured home community. The short layout caters to community residents seeking a quick, low-key round in a friendly Polk County setting.
notes: City listed as Polk County; actual city is Auburndale. 9-hole executive course

description: Streamsong Red and Blue are two of America's most acclaimed public-access courses, built on a former phosphate strip mine in Hardee County — Red designed by Coore & Crenshaw and Blue by Tom Doak, both opening in 2012. The moonscape-like sandy ridges and expansive skies of the reclaimed phosphate land create links-style conditions unlike anything else in the South.
notes: Nationally ranked top-30 public courses; Coore & Crenshaw (Red) and Tom Doak (Blue)

description: Streamsong Black is Gil Hanse's addition to the Streamsong Resort campus, opening in 2017 and earning Golf Digest's Best New Course of the Year award. Like its siblings, the Black route exploits the old phosphate terrain — but Hanse's routing leans into the wildest, most tumbling contours of the property for a distinctly rugged feel.
notes: Golf Digest Best New Course 2018; Gil Hanse design

description: River Ranch Golf Course is a 9-hole public layout at Westgate River Ranch Resort in far eastern Polk County, opened around 2005 on a sprawling 1,700-acre dude ranch. The course offers a relaxed short game experience as one of many resort amenities for guests of this western-themed getaway.
notes: City listed as Polk County; actual community is River Ranch. 9-hole resort course

description: The Country Club of Sebring is an 18-hole Ron Garl design from 1985, uniquely situated adjacent to and surrounded on three sides by Highlands Hammock State Park. The natural Florida scrub and old-growth hammock setting gives the course a character distinct from most residential country clubs in Highlands County.
notes: Ron Garl design; borders Highlands Hammock State Park

description: Turtle Run at Sun 'N Lake Golf Club is an 18-hole Charles Ankrom design opened in 1999, the newer of the two courses at the Sun 'N Lake facility in Sebring. The layout features narrow landing zones, moguls, and deep sand traps that reward precision over power.
notes: Charles Ankrom design; part of Sun 'N Lake Golf Club

description: Deer Run at Sun 'N Lake Golf Club is an 18-hole public course that opened in 1976 as the original layout of the Sun 'N Lake facility in Sebring. The course has been a Highlands County staple for nearly 50 years, offering open daily-fee play alongside the Turtle Run Course.
notes: Part of Sun 'N Lake Golf Club; 36-hole facility with Turtle Run
9-hole par-3 course; listed as CLOSED as of 2026 on Yelp

description: Sun 'N Lake Golf Course is the 36-hole public facility in Sebring encompassing both the Deer Run (1976) and Turtle Run (1999) courses. Managed by the Sun 'N Lake of Sebring Improvement District, the complex is the primary public golf destination for Highlands County golfers.
notes: This entry appears to represent the overall Sun N Lake facility (both Deer Run and Turtle Run); 36-hole public complex

description: Crystal Lake Club is a private 18-hole executive course (par 60) built in 1977 within a 55-plus manufactured home community in Avon Park. The compact layout is reserved for community residents and their overnight guests, offering a quick round in a peaceful lakeside setting.
notes: City listed as Sebring; actual city is Avon Park. Par-60 executive course; residents and overnight guests only

description: Stonecrest Golf Club is a Steve Nugent-designed par-72 course opened in 1993 within the Stonecrest active-adult community in Summerfield, Marion County. The 18-hole layout is open to outside play alongside its resident membership, and the club recently rebranded as Broad Stripes Golf & Social Club.
notes: Also operating as Broad Stripes Golf & Social Club; Steve Nugent design

description: Miona Lake Golf Club is a public 18-hole course in Wildwood, Sumter County, built by a local group of golfers in 1966. The tree-lined layout with narrow fairways and small greens has served the community for nearly 60 years and sits a short drive from The Villages.
notes: City listed as Sumter County; actual city is Wildwood. Course listed as closed on some sources — verify status

description: Palmer Legends Country Club is a 27-hole Arnold Palmer Signature design that opened in 2004 as one of The Villages' two championship-length private country clubs. The three distinct nines — Cherry Hill, Laurel Valley, and Riley Grove — provide a full-length challenge accessible exclusively to Villages residents.
notes: 27-hole Arnold Palmer design; one of two championship clubs in The Villages

description: One of The Villages' resident-only executive courses set within the sprawling retirement community spanning Lake, Marion, and Sumter counties in Central Florida. The layout provides a short, relaxed round designed for daily play and is accessible exclusively to Village residents and their registered guests.
notes: The Villages resident-only executive course

description: Evans Prairie Country Club is a 27-hole Arnold Palmer-designed championship facility that opened in fall 2012 as one of The Villages' two full-length country clubs. The Killdeer, Osprey, and Sandhill nines offer Villages residents a premium regulation golf experience in the newer southern portion of the community.
notes: 27-hole Arnold Palmer design; one of two championship clubs in The Villages; zip 32163 for newer southern section

description: One of The Villages' resident-only executive courses set within the sprawling retirement community spanning Lake, Marion, and Sumter counties in Central Florida. The layout provides a short, relaxed round designed for daily play and is accessible exclusively to Village residents and their registered guests.
notes: The Villages resident-only executive course

description: Nancy Lopez Legacy Golf Course is a public-access course in The Villages named in honor of Hall of Fame golfer Nancy Lopez, who grew up in the region. The course offers daily-fee play open to residents and visitors, distinct from the resident-only executive courses that dominate The Villages golf portfolio.
notes: Public-access course; named for Nancy Lopez

description: One of The Villages' resident-only executive courses set within the sprawling retirement community spanning Lake, Marion, and Sumter counties in Central Florida. The layout provides a short, relaxed round designed for daily play and is accessible exclusively to Village residents and their registered guests.

The layout provides a short, relaxed round designed for daily play and is accessible exclusively to Village residents and their registered guests.
notes: The Villages resident-only executive course

description: The Country Club of Winter Haven is one of Polk County's oldest and most prestigious private clubs, founded in 1923 on the shores of one of Winter Haven's many lakes. The 18-hole layout has served generations of members and stands as a pillar of the city's social and sporting life.
notes: Private; one of Florida's historic clubs founded 1923

description: Cypresswood Golf & Country Club is a Ron Garl-designed 18-hole course from 1973 in Winter Haven, open to both members and the public. Wide fairways and multiple tee positions make the layout approachable for all skill levels, while the full club amenities — pool, tennis, fitness — serve a broader country club membership.
notes: Ron Garl design; semi-private with public play available
